items.title: VoltAgent/awesome-ai-agent-papers — 2026 AI Agent Paper Curation
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: This week on arxiv, AI agent and LLM research continues at a high pace across several key themes. Major survey papers consolidate current understanding: a comprehensive survey on LLM agent methodology, applications, and challenges (2503.21460) frames goal-driven LLM agents as a pathway toward AGI, while a parallel review comparing 60+ benchmarks from 2019–2025 traces the arc from LLM reasoning to fully autonomous agents (2504.19678). A dedicated survey on AI agent communication protocols (2504.1

items.title: OpenAI, not yet public, raises $3B from retail investors in monster $122B fund raise | TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: AI startup funding activity in early 2026 has been dominated by massive rounds at the infrastructure and foundation model layer. OpenAI raised $110 billion in one of the largest private funding rounds in history, backed by Amazon ($50B), Nvidia ($30B), and SoftBank ($30B) at a $730B pre-money valuation — and subsequently closed an additional $3B from retail investors, bringing its total raise to $122B at an $852B valuation. xAI also closed a $20B Series E led by Valor Equity Partners, Fidelity,

items.title: langchain-ai/deepagents — Agent harness with LangChain/LangGraph
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: This week on GitHub, AI agent frameworks and tooling are dominating the trending charts. Key breakout repositories include langchain-ai/deepagents — a new agent harness built on LangChain/LangGraph with planning, filesystem backend, and subagent spawning capabilities — and microsoft/agent-framework, Microsoft's multi-language (Python/.NET) framework for building and orchestrating multi-agent workflows. items.title: DeepSeek V3.2 vs Llama 4 vs Qwen 3: Best Open-Source LLM for Production 2026
items.author: www.spheron.network
items.snippet: The open-source LLM landscape in early 2026 has seen a surge of competitive releases with strong benchmark showings. Three families dominate the conversation: Meta's Llama 4 (Scout, Maverick, Behemoth variants), Alibaba's Qwen 3, and DeepSeek V3.2. Llama 4 Maverick leads on raw MMLU at 85.5% and offers an unmatched 10M token context window in the Scout variant, while Qwen 3 235B leads on GPQA Diamond (77.2%) and AIME '24 math reasoning (85.7%), making it the strongest open-weight model for math

items.title: New Cursor Interface (3.0) · Cursor
items.author: cursor.com
items.snippet: Cursor released version 3.0 on April 2, 2026, representing a major overhaul centered around parallel agent execution. The new interface allows running many agents simultaneously across repos and environments — locally, in worktrees, in the cloud, and over SSH. Standout features include Design Mode (annotate and target UI elements in the browser for agents), Agent Tabs (side-by-side multi-chat view), a /worktree command for isolated git branch execution, and a /best-of-n command that runs the sam

items.title: Microsoft Build 2025: The age of AI agents and building the open agentic web - The Official Microsoft Blog
items.author: blogs.microsoft.com
items.snippet: Microsoft has been rapidly expanding its Copilot AI features across consumer and enterprise products. In early 2026, Microsoft announced Wave 3 of Microsoft 365 Copilot, introducing expanded model diversity including Claude and next-generation OpenAI models, along with new agentic experiences in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook. items.title: Agentic AI, MCP, and spec-driven development: Top blog posts of 2025 - The GitHub Blog
items.author: github.blog
items.snippet: GitHub has significantly expanded its AI agent capabilities through several major announcements. In May 2025, GitHub introduced the Copilot Coding Agent — embedded directly into GitHub — which activates when you assign a GitHub Issue to Copilot or prompt it in VS Code. It spins up a secure, customizable development environment via GitHub Actions and can autonomously fix bugs, implement features, and iterate on its own code. A Squad feature was also introduced to enable coordinated multi-agent wo

items.title: Introducing TRIBE v2: A Predictive Foundation Model Trained to Understand How the Human Brain Processes Complex Stimuli
items.author: ai.meta.com
items.snippet: Meta's most significant recent open source model release is the Llama 4 family, announced in early 2026. The Llama 4 herd introduces natively multimodal models built on a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture — including Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, both available for download on llama.com and Hugging Face. These are the first open-weight models from Meta with native multimodality and support for over 200 languages, with 10x more multilingual tokens than Llama 3. Meta also previewed Llama
